name: Nenets Autonomous Okrug (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the polar sky and the seas of the Arctic
the tundra vegetation and the forest belt

description: A horizontal triband of white, blue and green in proportions 17:5:3; a stylised blue Nenets ornament evoking reindeer antlers runs diagonally across the white stripe.
history: Adopted on 24 December 2007 by the Assembly of Deputies of the Nenets Autonomous Okrug.

features.symbolism: white represents the snow and ice of the tundra; blue stands for the polar sky and the seas; green for the brief vegetation of the tundra and the spruce forests.
a traditional Nenets ornament reading 'rogá olenya' (reindeer antlers), running along the white stripe diagonally.
